register.note: Full-width max-w-7xl loss-surface portrait with a mono figcaption is far more canvas-first than Ciechanowski's small floating watch, but at thumbnail scale it collapses to a red gradient with a faint line, so it is not yet poster-legible in isolation.

Unlike Seeing-Theory's squares visibly shrinking onto the line, the hero's start dot sits beside the bright minimum and the path then travels into darker high-loss terrain ending at an off-floor open ring, so 'the walk descends to the minimum' misreads from the pixels — held below floor on the trajectory, not the render.

Distill captions every regime inside the graphic; our hero stays bare (start/end/minimum unlabeled) with meaning in adjacent prose, though the experiment's surface view does carry in-graphic 'x valley floor (OLS)' and 'start' labels.

The staircase is fixed — the hero shows smooth continuous contour ellipses and constant ~1.5px anti-aliased lines that thin on the valley floor, putting the surface in the same buttery finish class as Ciechanowski's spring strokes; the See-it left-column dead air and floating 'N' avatar remain but are not point-costing.

From statics: one explanatory ease-out hero path-draw and a fully learner-controlled training loop (Play/scrub on a log-loss axis), so motion is transitional and steppable, exceeding 3B1B's non-interactive frame on control.

Semantic grammar holds lab-wide and into AA-tinted, underlined prose (gold data, blue prediction, purple eta/path, red reserved for loss/'climb'); docked from 4 because the all-red hero band field uses error-red as ambient terrain, mildly diluting red's 'error' meaning.

hero.note: Full-width smooth-rendered loss surface with one ease-out load motion and a mono figcaption, but it carries no in-graphic labels and the descent visibly wanders off the bright minimum into higher-loss terrain, so the mechanism does not read at hero or thumbnail scale.

assessment.note: Explain is a graded instrument with a live coordinated descent widget the learner answers against, plus interactive predict-and-verify items, though the explicitly-labelled transfer item is itself a 3-option MCQ.

blocking: Make the hero descent visibly terminate AT the bright minimum/x — start the path in a dark high-loss corner and have it converge onto the valley floor, so 'descends to the minimum' reads without prior knowledge.
Carry the experiment's in-graphic labels ('x the valley floor', 'start') into the bare hero, plus a small loss legend, so meaning stops living only in adjacent prose.
verdict.summary: Atmosphere-finish now clears floor 3 — the loss-surface staircase is genuinely fixed and the render is Distill-soft — but mechanism-in-the-picture stays at 2 because the hero descent wanders off the minimum into higher-loss terrain, so the exhibit holds.
